ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling food product class; no specific mixed-assortment party platter class exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers dumplings generically; no mixed-assortment platter subcode exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for mixed dumpling platter; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard party platter serving is 30 pieces weighing approximately 1000 g, typically shared among 4–6 people at a meal.

nutrient_source.title: Dumplings (Jiaozi), pork and vegetable filling — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo Database entry for pork-and-vegetable dumplings/jiaozi. Secondary nutrients (dietary_fibre_g, saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for dumplings (pork and vegetable) where HPB data was unavailable. Values represent a mixed assortment platter; individual variety composition may vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for mixed dumpling party platter. GI of 48 estimated using wheat-flour dumpling (jiaozi/wonton) as the closest analogous food from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Wheat-flour dumplings with meat-and-vegetable filling have a reported GI of approximately 46–50 (low category). GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 48 × (8.03 g/100g × 1000 g serving) / 100 = 48 × 80.3 / 100 ≈ 38.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100 g with modest potassium at 82 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: The intrinsic sodium content of 46 mg/100 g is well below the 200 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension concern; however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chili sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.03 g/100 g), low GI (~48), high protein (8.71 g/100 g) moderating glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of approximately 48 and only 8 g of carbohydrate per 100 g, mixed dumplings produce a moderate glycaemic load per platter; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should practise portion control, avoid sweet dipping sauces, and account for the refined wheat flour wrapper contribution to total carbohydrate intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (4.0 g/100 g), low sodium (46 mg/100 g), low trans fat (0.05 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100 g is below the 5 g/100 g threshold of concern and trans fat is negligible at 0.05 g/100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to manage cumulative saturated fat intake and avoid sodium-rich dipping sauces to protect blood pressure targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork filling contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6); anti-inflammatory ginger and garlic present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ginger and garlic in the filling provide anti-inflammatory benefit, but pork-derived arachidonic acid may amplify inflammatory pathways; patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit portion size and request reduced-sodium soy sauce to avoid sodium-driven tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mixed Dumpling Party Platter (什锦饺子拼盘) is a Singaporean-Chinese sharing dish consisting of 30 pieces of assorted dumplings served on a single platter, typically weighing approximately 1000 g. The dumplings feature thin wheat-flour wrappers encasing a savoury filling of minced pork, cabbage, chives, ginger, garlic, and soy sauce, and may be prepared by pan-frying (producing a crisp golden base), steaming (yielding a soft, translucent skin), or boiling. The texture contrast — crisp or tender exterior with a juicy, flavourful interior — is a defining characteristic. Nutritionally, the platter provides 101 kcal per 100 g, with a notably high protein content of 8.71 g/100 g that moderates gastric emptying and attenuates postprandial glucose response. Carbohydrate is low at 8.03 g/100 g with minimal sugar (0.21 g/100 g), and sodium is very low at 46 mg/100 g intrinsically, though dipping sauces can significantly increase sodium load. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 48 (low category), making this a suitable option for individuals managing blood glucose. Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.05 g/100 g are within acceptable ranges for cardiovascular health. The dish is culturally associated with Chinese New Year celebrations, family gatherings, and casual dining occasions in Singapore, and is commonly served at lunch or dinner. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or soy allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a dumpling party platter?
What is the GI of dumplings?
What is 什锦饺子拼盘?
Is dumpling platter suitable for hypertension?
How many pieces in a dumpling party platter?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo Database with high confidence (0.95). Secondary nutrients (dietary_fibre_g, saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg) adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as HPB did not report these fields, reducing overall confidence to medium. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat-flour dumplings, not a direct measurement of this specific platter product.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for mixed dumpling platter not available in HPB Singapore Foodo Database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo Database entry for dumplings and are not available in USDA SR Legacy for this specific mixed-assortment product as of 2026. saturated_fat_g value of 4.0 g/100 g is noted as unusual relative to total fat of 3.78 g/100 g (saturated fat exceeds total fat); this discrepancy is preserved from the original HPB/GrabFood source data and flagged for source-level review — the saturated_fat_g value may reflect a data entry error in the original record. Food subgroup corrected from 'Baking ingredients' to 'Dumplings and dim sum' as the original subgroup was clearly misclassified. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour dumpling entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. GL per serving = 48 × 80.3 / 100 ≈ 38.5 (based on 1000 g serving containing 80.3 g carbohydrate). Iteration 3 fix: verified all aliases_by_language string values are present verbatim in the flat aliases array. The Hindi alias 'मिश्रित डम्पलिंग पार्टी थाली' was corrected in the flat aliases array (previous iteration had a mixed-script typo 'मिश्रित डम्पलिंग पार्टी थाली' vs 'मिश्रित डम्पलिंग पार्टी थाली'); all 14 flat alias entries now exactly match their aliases_by_language counterparts.
